consensus.h(3) Library Functions Manual consensus.h(3)

NAME

consensus.h - Mode-agnostic ring consensus abstraction for session discovery.

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct session_consensus_callbacks_t
Callbacks for consensus operations.

Typedefs


typedef asciichat_error_t(* session_consensus_send_packet_fn) (void *context, const uint8_t next_participant_id[16], const uint8_t *packet, size_t packet_size)
Callback: Send a consensus packet to the next ring participant. typedef asciichat_error_t(* session_consensus_on_election_fn) (void *context, const uint8_t host_id[16], const char host_address[64], uint16_t host_port, const uint8_t backup_id[16], const char backup_address[64], uint16_t backup_port)
Callback: Handle elected host result. typedef asciichat_error_t(* session_consensus_get_metrics_fn) (void *context, const uint8_t my_id[16], participant_metrics_t *out_metrics)
Callback: Measure current participant's network metrics. typedef asciichat_error_t(* session_consensus_election_fn) (void *context, const participant_metrics_t *metrics, int num_metrics, int *out_best_index, int *out_backup_index)
Callback type for custom election algorithm.

Functions


asciichat_error_t session_consensus_create (const uint8_t my_id[16], bool is_leader, const uint8_t participant_ids[64][16], int num_participants, const session_consensus_callbacks_t *callbacks, session_consensus_t **out_consensus)
Create a new session consensus instance. void session_consensus_destroy (session_consensus_t *consensus)
Destroy consensus instance and free all resources. asciichat_error_t session_consensus_process (session_consensus_t *consensus, uint32_t timeout_ms)
Main consensus processing loop - call periodically. asciichat_error_t session_consensus_set_topology (session_consensus_t *consensus, const uint8_t participant_ids[64][16], int num_participants)
Update ring topology when participants change. asciichat_error_t session_consensus_on_collection_start (session_consensus_t *consensus, uint32_t round_id, uint64_t deadline_ns)
Handle incoming STATS_COLLECTION_START packet. asciichat_error_t session_consensus_on_stats_update (session_consensus_t *consensus, const uint8_t sender_id[16], const participant_metrics_t *metrics, uint8_t num_metrics)
Handle incoming STATS_UPDATE packet (metrics relayed around ring). asciichat_error_t session_consensus_on_election_result (session_consensus_t *consensus, const uint8_t host_id[16], const char host_address[64], uint16_t host_port, const uint8_t backup_id[16], const char backup_address[64], uint16_t backup_port)
Handle incoming ELECTION_RESULT packet from leader. asciichat_error_t session_consensus_get_elected_host (session_consensus_t *consensus, uint8_t out_host_id[16], char out_host_address[64], uint16_t *out_host_port, uint8_t out_backup_id[16], char out_backup_address[64], uint16_t *out_backup_port)
Get the currently elected host. bool session_consensus_is_ready (session_consensus_t *consensus)
Check if consensus has completed at least one round. int session_consensus_get_state (session_consensus_t *consensus)
Get current consensus state. uint64_t session_consensus_time_until_next_round (session_consensus_t *consensus)
Get time until next consensus round in nanoseconds. int session_consensus_get_metrics_count (session_consensus_t *consensus)
Get count of metrics collected in current round.

Detailed Description

Mode-agnostic ring consensus abstraction for session discovery.

Provides a clean abstraction over the ring consensus protocol that any mode (server, client, acds, discovery) can use. The abstraction:

  • Wraps all consensus modules (state, coordinator, topology, election, metrics)
  • Uses callbacks to decouple consensus logic from transport/metrics specifics
  • Provides non-blocking periodic processing
  • Handles automatic packet generation and state transitions

Design Principles:

1.
Consensus algorithm is mode-agnostic - doesn't know about TCP/WebRTC/etc
2.
Modes provide callbacks for:
•
Sending packets to next participant
•
Measuring network metrics (NAT quality, bandwidth, etc)
•
Handling elected host results
3.
No tight coupling to specific transports or capture mechanisms
4.
Each mode can opt-in or opt-out independently

Definition in file consensus.h.

Typedef Documentation

typedef asciichat_error_t(* session_consensus_election_fn) (void *context, const participant_metrics_t *metrics, int num_metrics, int *out_best_index, int *out_backup_index)

Callback type for custom election algorithm. If provided, consensus calls this instead of built-in election. Allows modes to implement custom host selection logic.

Parameters

context Opaque pointer provided by caller during create
metrics Array of participant metrics
num_metrics Count of metrics
out_best_index Output: Index of best host (not ID)
out_backup_index Output: Index of backup host

Returns

ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise

Definition at line 110 of file consensus.h.

typedef asciichat_error_t(* session_consensus_get_metrics_fn) (void *context, const uint8_t my_id[16], participant_metrics_t *out_metrics)

Callback: Measure current participant's network metrics. Called by consensus to collect metrics for this participant. Should measure:

  • NAT tier (0=LAN, 1=Public, 2=UPnP, 3=STUN, 4=TURN)
  • Upload bandwidth (Kbps)
  • RTT to current host (nanoseconds)
  • STUN probe success rate (0-100%)
  • Public address and port
  • Connection type (Direct, UPnP, STUN, TURN)

Parameters

context Opaque pointer provided by caller during create
my_id 16-byte UUID of this participant
out_metrics Output metrics structure (caller-allocated)

Returns

ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise

Definition at line 94 of file consensus.h.

typedef asciichat_error_t(* session_consensus_on_election_fn) (void *context, const uint8_t host_id[16], const char host_address[64], uint16_t host_port, const uint8_t backup_id[16], const char backup_address[64], uint16_t backup_port)

Callback: Handle elected host result. Called by consensus when a new host election is complete and announced. The mode should:

  • Store the elected host and backup host info
  • Update connection targets if participant
  • Initiate host role if elected as new host

Parameters

context Opaque pointer provided by caller during create
host_id 16-byte UUID of elected host
host_address Address to connect to (hostname or IP)
host_port Port to connect to
backup_id 16-byte UUID of backup host
backup_address Backup address
backup_port Backup port

Returns

ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise

Definition at line 73 of file consensus.h.

typedef asciichat_error_t(* session_consensus_send_packet_fn) (void *context, const uint8_t next_participant_id[16], const uint8_t *packet, size_t packet_size)

Callback: Send a consensus packet to the next ring participant. Called by consensus to send packets around the ring. The mode is responsible for serializing and transmitting to the appropriate transport.

Parameters

context Opaque pointer provided by caller during create
next_participant_id 16-byte UUID of next participant in ring
packet Packet data to send (including header)
packet_size Size of packet in bytes

Returns

ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code if sending failed

Definition at line 52 of file consensus.h.

Function Documentation

asciichat_error_t session_consensus_create (const uint8_t my_id[16], bool is_leader, const uint8_t participant_ids[64][16], int num_participants, const session_consensus_callbacks_t * callbacks, session_consensus_t ** out_consensus)

Create a new session consensus instance. Initializes consensus for a participant in a session. The consensus will manage ring topology, metrics collection, and host election according to the configured ring parameters.

Parameters

my_id 16-byte UUID of this participant
is_leader Whether this participant should run elections (typically last in ring)
participant_ids Array of participant UUIDs (up to 64)
num_participants Count of participants
callbacks Callback functions for mode integration
out_consensus Output consensus handle (caller must destroy)

Returns

ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise

Definition at line 108 of file consensus.c.

111                                                                                 {
112   if (!my_id || !callbacks || !out_consensus) {
113     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters: my_id=%p, callbacks=%p, out_consensus=%p", my_id,
114                      callbacks, out_consensus);
115   }
116 
117   if (!callbacks->send_packet || !callbacks->on_election || !callbacks->get_metrics) {
118     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Required callbacks missing: send_packet=%p, on_election=%p, get_metrics=%p",
119                      callbacks->send_packet, callbacks->on_election, callbacks->get_metrics);
120   }
121 
122   if (num_participants < 1 || num_participants > 64) {
123     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid participant count: %d", num_participants);
124   }
125 
126   // Allocate consensus handle
127   session_consensus_t *consensus = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(session_consensus_t), session_consensus_t *);
128   if (!consensus) {
129     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate session consensus");
130   }
131 
132   // Copy identity
133   memcpy(consensus->my_id, my_id, 16);
134   consensus->is_leader = is_leader;
135 
136   // Copy callbacks
137   consensus->send_packet = callbacks->send_packet;
138   consensus->on_election = callbacks->on_election;
139   consensus->get_metrics = callbacks->get_metrics;
140   consensus->election = callbacks->election; // May be NULL
141   consensus->context = callbacks->context;
142 
143   // Create topology
144   asciichat_error_t err = consensus_topology_create(participant_ids, num_participants, my_id, &consensus->topology);
145   if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
146     SAFE_FREE(consensus);
147     return err;
148   }
149 
150   // Create coordinator
151   err = consensus_coordinator_create(my_id, consensus->topology, session_consensus_election_bridge, consensus,
152                                      &consensus->coordinator);
153   if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
154     consensus_topology_destroy(consensus->topology);
155     SAFE_FREE(consensus);
156     return err;
157   }
158 
159   log_debug("Session consensus created: my_id=%.*s, is_leader=%d, participants=%d", 16, my_id, is_leader,
160             num_participants);
161 
162   *out_consensus = consensus;
163   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
164 }




References consensus_coordinator_create(),
    consensus_topology_create(), consensus_topology_destroy(),
    session_consensus::context,
    session_consensus_callbacks_t::context,
    session_consensus::coordinator, session_consensus::election,
    session_consensus_callbacks_t::election,
    session_consensus::get_metrics,
    session_consensus_callbacks_t::get_metrics,
    session_consensus::is_leader, session_consensus::my_id,
    session_consensus::on_election,
    session_consensus_callbacks_t::on_election, SAFE_FREE,
    session_consensus::send_packet,
    session_consensus_callbacks_t::send_packet, SET_ERRNO, and
    session_consensus::topology.

void
  session_consensus_destroy (session_consensus_t * consensus)


Destroy consensus instance and free all resources.


Parameters


consensus Consensus handle (safe to pass
  NULL)


Definition at line 166 of file consensus.c.


166                                                                {
167   if (!consensus) {
168     return;
169   }
170 
171   if (consensus->coordinator) {
172     consensus_coordinator_destroy(consensus->coordinator);
173   }
174 
175   if (consensus->topology) {
176     consensus_topology_destroy(consensus->topology);
177   }
178 
179   SAFE_FREE(consensus);
180 }






References consensus_coordinator_destroy(),
    consensus_topology_destroy(), session_consensus::coordinator,
    SAFE_FREE, and session_consensus::topology.






asciichat_error_t
  session_consensus_get_elected_host (session_consensus_t * consensus,
  uint8_t out_host_id[16], char out_host_address[64], uint16_t * out_host_port,
  uint8_t out_backup_id[16], char out_backup_address[64], uint16_t *
  out_backup_port)


Get the currently elected host. Returns the most recently elected
    host and backup. If no election has completed yet, returns the previous
    stored result if available, or error.




Parameters


consensus Consensus handle


out_host_id Output: 16-byte UUID of host (caller provides buffer)


out_host_address Output: Host address (caller provides buffer, 64 bytes)


out_host_port Output: Host port


out_backup_id Output: 16-byte UUID of backup (caller provides buffer)


out_backup_address Output: Backup address (caller provides buffer, 64
  bytes)


out_backup_port Output: Backup port


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


Definition at line 259 of file consensus.c.


262                                                                                 {
263   if (!consensus || !out_host_id || !out_host_address || !out_host_port || !out_backup_id || !out_backup_address ||
264       !out_backup_port) {
265     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters");
266   }
267 
268   // Get from coordinator
269   asciichat_error_t err = consensus_coordinator_get_current_host(consensus->coordinator, out_host_id, out_backup_id);
270   if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
271     return err;
272   }
273 
274   // NOTE: The current API doesn't return addresses/ports. This is a limitation
275   // of the coordinator that should be addressed in a future enhancement.
276   // For now, return zeros and indicate this is a placeholder.
277   memset(out_host_address, 0, 64);
278   *out_host_port = 0;
279   memset(out_backup_address, 0, 64);
280   *out_backup_port = 0;
281 
282   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
283 }






References consensus_coordinator_get_current_host(),
    session_consensus::coordinator, and SET_ERRNO.

bool session_consensus_is_ready
  (session_consensus_t * consensus)


Check if consensus has completed at least one round. Returns true
    if at least one complete consensus round has finished with a valid elected
    host and backup.




Parameters


consensus Consensus handle


Returns


true if consensus reached, false otherwise


Definition at line 285 of file consensus.c.


285                                                                 {
286   if (!consensus) {
287     return false;
288   }
289 
290   // Check if we have a valid elected host
291   uint8_t host_id[16];
292   uint8_t backup_id[16];
293   asciichat_error_t err = consensus_coordinator_get_current_host(consensus->coordinator, host_id, backup_id);
294 
295   return err == ASCIICHAT_OK;
296 }

asciichat_error_t
  session_consensus_on_collection_start (session_consensus_t * consensus,
  uint32_t round_id, uint64_t deadline_ns)


Handle incoming STATS_COLLECTION_START packet. Consensus calls
    this when it receives a collection start from the previous ring participant.
    Should be called from packet handler in mode.




Parameters


consensus Consensus handle


round_id Collection round ID


deadline_ns Unix nanosecond deadline for collection


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


Definition at line 218 of file consensus.c.

221     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "consensus is NULL");
222   }
223 
224   return consensus_coordinator_on_collection_start(consensus->coordinator, round_id, deadline_ns);
225 }

asciichat_error_t
  session_consensus_on_election_result (session_consensus_t * consensus,
  const uint8_t host_id[16], const char host_address[64], uint16_t host_port,
  const uint8_t backup_id[16], const char backup_address[64], uint16_t
  backup_port)


Handle incoming ELECTION_RESULT packet from leader. Consensus
    calls this when leader announces the elected host. Should be called from
    packet handler in mode.




Parameters


consensus Consensus handle


host_id 16-byte UUID of elected host


host_address Address of elected host


host_port Port of elected host


backup_id 16-byte UUID of backup host


backup_address Address of backup host


backup_port Port of backup host


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


Definition at line 236 of file consensus.c.


239                                                                              {
240   if (!consensus || !host_id || !backup_id) {
241     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters");
242   }
243 
244   // First acknowledge the election in coordinator
245   asciichat_error_t err = consensus_coordinator_on_election_result(consensus->coordinator, host_id, backup_id);
246   if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
247     return err;
248   }
249 
250   // Then call the mode's election callback
251   if (consensus->on_election) {
252     return consensus->on_election(consensus->context, host_id, host_address, host_port, backup_id, backup_address,
253                                   backup_port);
254   }
255 
256   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
257 }






References consensus_coordinator_on_election_result(),
    session_consensus::context, session_consensus::coordinator,
    session_consensus::on_election, and SET_ERRNO.






asciichat_error_t
  session_consensus_on_stats_update (session_consensus_t * consensus,
  const uint8_t sender_id[16], const participant_metrics_t * metrics, uint8_t
  num_metrics)


Handle incoming STATS_UPDATE packet (metrics relayed around ring).
    Consensus calls this when it receives metrics from the previous participant.
    Should be called from packet handler in mode.




Parameters


consensus Consensus handle


sender_id 16-byte UUID of who sent this update


metrics Array of metrics in this packet


num_metrics Count of metrics


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


Definition at line 227 of file consensus.c.


228                                                                                                                {
229   if (!consensus || !sender_id || !metrics) {
230     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters");
231   }
232 
233   return consensus_coordinator_on_stats_update(consensus->coordinator, sender_id, metrics, num_metrics);
234 }






References consensus_coordinator_on_stats_update(),
    session_consensus::coordinator, and SET_ERRNO.






asciichat_error_t
  session_consensus_process (session_consensus_t * consensus, uint32_t
  timeout_ms)


Main consensus processing loop - call periodically. Handles:


    
  
  • Round scheduling (every 5 minutes)
    • 
  
  • Collection deadline enforcement (30 seconds)
    • 
  
  • Metrics measurement and relay around ring
    • 
  
  • Election computation (leader only)
    • 
  
  • Result broadcasting
    • 



Non-blocking: returns immediately if no action needed. Modes
    should call this regularly (at least once per second) to ensure timely round
    scheduling and deadline enforcement.




Parameters


consensus Consensus handle


timeout_ms Timeout for this call in milliseconds (0 = non-blocking)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


Definition at line 182 of file consensus.c.


182                                                                                                  {
183   if (!consensus) {
184     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "consensus is NULL");
185   }
186 
187   // Call coordinator process
188   return consensus_coordinator_process(consensus->coordinator, timeout_ms);
189 }






References consensus_coordinator_process(),
    session_consensus::coordinator, and SET_ERRNO.






asciichat_error_t
  session_consensus_set_topology (session_consensus_t * consensus, const
  uint8_t participant_ids[64][16], int num_participants)


Update ring topology when participants change. Called when the
    ring topology changes (participants join/leave). Updates the consensus
    topology and resets any in-progress round.




Parameters


consensus Consensus handle


participant_ids Updated array of participant UUIDs


num_participants New count of participants


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


Definition at line 191 of file consensus.c.


192                                                                        {
193   if (!consensus || !participant_ids) {
194     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters");
195   }
196 
197   if (num_participants < 1 || num_participants > 64) {
198     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid participant count: %d", num_participants);
199   }
200 
201   // Destroy old topology
202   if (consensus->topology) {
203     consensus_topology_destroy(consensus->topology);
204   }
205 
206   // Create new topology
207   asciichat_error_t err =
208       consensus_topology_create(participant_ids, num_participants, consensus->my_id, &consensus->topology);
209   if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
210     consensus->topology = NULL;
211     return err;
212   }
213 
214   // Update coordinator with new topology
215   return consensus_coordinator_on_ring_members(consensus->coordinator, consensus->topology);
216 }






References consensus_coordinator_on_ring_members(),
    consensus_topology_create(), consensus_topology_destroy(),
    session_consensus::coordinator, session_consensus::my_id,
    SET_ERRNO, and session_consensus::topology.






uint64_t
  session_consensus_time_until_next_round (session_consensus_t *
  consensus)


Get time until next consensus round in nanoseconds. Useful for
    scheduling when to call process() next.




Parameters


consensus Consensus handle


Returns


Nanoseconds until next round, or 0 if round is due
  now
